# Release Checklist

Use this for every npm publish â€” patch, minor, or major. Work top to bottom; don't skip steps.

`main` is protected â€” all release work lands via PR, never a direct push. npm publish is **not** triggered by merging to `main`; it only fires when a GitHub Release is published (`.github/workflows/publish.yml`, `on: release: types: [published]`). The gh-pages site, by contrast, redeploys on **every** push to `main` (`.github/workflows/gh-pages.yml`) â€” so by the time you publish to npm, the docs site has already been live with the matching version for as long as the PR has been merged. Don't publish before that PR is merged; otherwise the npm package points at a docs site that hasn't caught up yet.

---

## 1. Pre-release â€” branch and PR

- [ ] Release work (version bump, changelog, any accompanying doc/site fixes) happens on a branch, not directly on `main`
- [ ] Branch is up to date with `main`: `git fetch && git status`
- [ ] No uncommitted changes: `git status`
- [ ] All CI checks green on the PR (tests, typecheck, lint, complexity, size, agent-docs)

---

## 2. Version bump

Decide the version bump following [SemVer](https://semver.org/):

| Change | Version |
|--------|---------|
| Breaking API change, removed feature | MAJOR (`X.0.0`) |
| New feature, new addon/handler (backward-compatible) | MINOR (`0.X.0`) |
| Bug fix, performance fix, docs (no API change) | PATCH (`0.0.X`) |

```bash
# In package.json â€” bump "version" manually, or:
npm version patch   # 2.1.0 â†’ 2.1.1
npm version minor   # 2.1.0 â†’ 2.2.0
npm version major   # 2.1.0 â†’ 3.0.0
```

> `npm version` creates a commit and tag automatically. If you prefer manual control, edit `package.json` directly and create the tag in step 8 (after merge, not before).

---

## 3. CHANGELOG.md

- [ ] Add a new section at the top of `CHANGELOG.md`:

```markdown
## [X.Y.Z] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- ...

### Fixed
- ...

### Performance
- ...

### Breaking Changes
- ...

### Tests
- X tests passing (from Y) | N new tests

### Documentation
- ...
```

Use `git log --oneline vPREV..HEAD` to enumerate commits since last release.

- [ ] Double-check the test count against `npm run test -- --run` output at the *end* of the branch, not whatever it was when you started writing the entry â€” it drifts if more commits land afterward.

---

## 4. Sync docs (facts + agent bundles)

`README.md` badges, `docs/README.md`'s version/size line, browser-support tables, and the `llms.txt` / `llms-full.txt` agent bundles are all generated from `package.json` and the built `dist/` â€” never hand-edit them.

```bash
npm run docs:sync
```

This rebuilds `dist/`, regenerates `docs/metrics.json` (version, gzipped sizes, test count), rewrites every `<!-- lume:* -->` marker region (README badges, hero line, entry table, `docs/README.md`'s version line, browser tables, nav footers, â€¦), and regenerates `llms.txt` / `llms-full.txt`.

- [ ] `npm run docs:sync` run after the version bump (step 2) and CHANGELOG entry (step 3)
- [ ] `git diff` reviewed â€” only expected files changed (marker regions, `docs/metrics.json`, `llms.txt`, `llms-full.txt`)

> The gh-pages **site** (`gh-pages/index.html`, `home.js`, `compare.js`) pulls version/size/ test-count from `gh-pages/src/data/metrics.json` automatically via `__LUME_VERSION__` / `__LUME_SIZE__` / `__LUME_TESTS__` placeholders, regenerated by `scripts/build-site-assets.js` on every site build â€” no manual edits needed there. If you ever hardcode a number directly into a `gh-pages/src/**` file instead of using a placeholder, it *will* go stale next release (this happened â€” see `compare.js`'s bundle size row and the footer tagline in `gh-pages/index.html`, both fixed to use `__LUME_SIZE__`).

**Not optional after a version bump:** the version and size figures baked into these files come from `package.json` and `dist/`, so bumping the version in step 2 makes them stale â€” the CI `agent-docs` job (`npm run docs:check`) fails the PR until `npm run docs:sync` is re-run. Commit the regenerated files with the release commit (step 6).

---

## 5. Build and validate

```bash
npm run validate
# Runs: build â†’ size check â†’ complexity â†’ lint â†’ typecheck â†’ coverage â†’ docs check
```

All steps must pass. If coverage drops from 100%, add tests before proceeding.

---

## 6. Commit release changes

```bash
git add package.json package-lock.json CHANGELOG.md README.md docs/README.md docs/metrics.json llms.txt llms-full.txt
git commit -m "chore(release): prepare vX.Y.Z"
```

Bundle this with any accompanying doc/nav/site fixes discovered while preparing the release, in the **same PR** â€” don't split "release commit" and "doc fixes" across two PRs. If they land separately, `main` sits mid-merge with the new version number but stale docs, and the gh-pages site redeploys that inconsistent state the moment the first PR merges (it deploys on every push to `main`, not just release-tagged ones).

---

## 7. Open PR, get it merged

- [ ] Open the PR against `main`
- [ ] CI green (`ci.yml` runs on PR)
- [ ] Merge

Merging triggers `gh-pages.yml` automatically â€” the docs site redeploys with the new version, corrected nav, and updated numbers. Confirm this before moving on:

- [ ] Actions tab â€” `Deploy GitHub Pages` workflow completed
- [ ] Spot-check `https://sathvikc.github.io/lume-js/` â€” version number and docs correct

Nothing is published to npm yet at this point â€” that's intentional.

---

## 8. Tag the release

From an up-to-date `main` (after the merge above, not before):

```bash
git checkout main && git pull
git tag vX.Y.Z
git push origin vX.Y.Z
```

> If you used `npm version` in step 2, the tag already exists on your branch â€” after the PR merges, re-tag the actual merge commit on `main` instead of pushing the branch's tag, unless the merge was a fast-forward.

---

## 9. Publish to npm â€” via GitHub Release, not manually

- [ ] Go to GitHub â†’ Releases â†’ Draft a new release
- [ ] Tag: `vX.Y.Z` (the tag you just pushed)
- [ ] Title: `Lume.js vX.Y.Z`
- [ ] Body: use `.github/RELEASE_TEMPLATE.md` as a starting point
- [ ] Publish release

Publishing the release fires `.github/workflows/publish.yml` (`on: release: types: [published]`), which re-runs `npm run validate` in CI and then `npm publish --provenance` â€” using the `NPM_TOKEN` secret, not your local npm login, and attaching a provenance attestation that links the package to this workflow run. **Don't run `npm publish` locally as the primary path** â€” it bypasses the CI validate gate and risks a double-publish (npm rejects the second attempt, but it's a confusing failure to debug).

Local `npm publish --dry-run` is still useful *before* tagging, to sanity-check what would ship. A local `npm publish` is a legitimate fallback only if the Action itself fails after you've already confirmed the dry run and validate are clean.

---

## 10. Verify npm + CDN propagation

- [ ] Check the Actions tab â€” `Publish to npm` workflow completed
- [ ] `npm view lume-js version` shows `X.Y.Z`

Wait ~5 minutes, then confirm:

- [ ] `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/lume-js@X.Y.Z/dist/index.mjs` resolves
- [ ] `https://unpkg.com/lume-js@X.Y.Z/dist/lume.global.js` resolves
- [ ] `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/lume-js@X.Y.Z/dist/addons.mjs` resolves
- [ ] `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/lume-js@X.Y.Z/dist/state.min.mjs` resolves (universal core)

---

## 11. Framework benchmarks (if applicable)

If this release affects observable performance, bundle size, or public API:

1. Wait for npm + CDN propagation (step 10) â€” the benchmark app pulls from the CDN, not a local build
2. Update the benchmark app's `lume-js` import to `@X.Y.Z`
3. Re-run the full benchmark suite
4. Update `summary.json` and `frameworks.json`
5. Commit: `chore(bench): update Lume.js to vX.Y.Z`

---

## Post-release

- [ ] Announce in relevant channels if a notable release
- [ ] Update any open issues that were resolved by this release
- [ ] If there are pre-release tags (`alpha`, `beta`, `rc`) on npm, deprecate them: `npm deprecate lume-js@X.Y.Z-beta.1 "Use vX.Y.Z"`
- [ ] Check for stale dist-tags pointing at old pre-releases (`npm dist-tag ls lume-js`) â€” remove or re-point them (`npm dist-tag rm lume-js next`); a stale `next` tag once served a year-old alpha to everyone following the install docs
